bugs.title: Chat history missing messages
bugs.description: GetChatHistory returns only 10 messages (5 user + 5 assistant) but 7 user messages were sent (6 counted by usage endpoint, plus 1 selection). Two planning-mode message pairs (Dubai fine dining + full itinerary request) are missing from Firestore chat history. Usage endpoint reports 6 used, confirming messages were processed by the server.
bugs.steps_to_reproduce: 1. Send 7 messages across selection, planning, companion modes in one session. 2. Call GetChatHistory with limit=50. 3. Only 5 user messages appear; 2 planning messages are missing.
bugs.expected: All 7 user messages and their assistant responses should appear in chat history (14 total messages).
bugs.actual: Only 10 messages returned (5 user + 5 assistant). Planning messages about Dubai dining and full itinerary are absent.

ux_issues.description: Booking.com recommended for ultra-luxury Maldives resort. For properties like Soneva Fushi, Waldorf Astoria Ithaafushi, and St. Regis, suggesting Booking.com is tone-deaf to the luxury segment. These resorts are typically booked directly or through luxury travel advisors for best rates, villa availability, and resort credits.
ux_issues.suggestion: For ultra-luxury properties (5-star island resorts, $1000+/night), the recommend_booking tool should note that direct resort booking or a luxury travel advisor may secure better rates and complimentary extras, rather than defaulting to Booking.com.

ux_issues.description: Day 8 itinerary has both a private desert safari (5-6 hours, ending with private dinner) AND Dinner at STAY by Yannick Alleno. These are incompatible -- the desert safari includes a bespoke dining component that replaces a restaurant dinner. The AI's description even says 'This experience replaces a formal restaurant dinner for the evening' but still scheduled the restaurant dinner on the same day.
ux_issues.suggestion: The AI should detect scheduling conflicts when one activity description explicitly states it replaces another planned activity type.

ai_behavior_issues.issue: Duplicate create_itinerary_items call in private dining response
ai_behavior_issues.context: After successfully creating 3 itinerary items for private dining, the AI called create_itinerary_items a second time with the exact same items. The dedup logic correctly returned 'already_exists' with 0 created, but the redundant call wastes tokens.

ai_behavior_issues.issue: Expert persona greeting references Paris despite being assigned to Maldives
ai_behavior_issues.context: The expert 'Isabelle' was handed off for romance/luxury/food in the Maldives context, but her greeting says 'Ah, Paris... the city whispers secrets of love and indulgence.' This is a persona composition issue -- the greeting appears to be a cached default for this expert rather than contextual to the Maldives.

ai_behavior_issues.issue: No mention of February weather advantages
ai_behavior_issues.context: The persona specifically notes February is dry season in Maldives and pleasant in Dubai (20-25C). The AI never confirmed this is excellent timing for both destinations, which is a missed opportunity for a luxury traveler who values details.

ai_behavior_issues.issue: Tresind Studio incorrectly located at One&Only Royal Mirage
ai_behavior_issues.context: The AI placed Tresind Studio at 'One&Only Royal Mirage, The Palm Jumeirah' in the itinerary. Tresind Studio is actually located in DIFC (Dubai International Financial Centre), not at the One&Only Royal Mirage. This is a factual error that could mislead travelers.

usefulness_evaluation.narrative: The AI demonstrated strong luxury calibration overall, recommending top-tier properties (Soneva Fushi, Waldorf Astoria, St. Regis) with specific differentiators (outdoor cinema, underwater restaurant, butler service). The 9-day itinerary had excellent luxury pacing with breathing room and private experiences. Expert handoff worked well for dining topics. Key weaknesses: Booking.com is wrong for ultra-luxury, one factual restaurant location error, and 2 chat messages went missing from history. The companion mode was concise and contextually aware. As Victoria, I would genuinely find this useful for planning, though I would want a travel advisor for the actual bookings.
